Your Personal Morning Dashboard

The Morning Briefing

Sunday, March 22, 2026
Top Story

Three weeks in, Iran war escalates beyond Trump's control

President Donald Trump ends the third week of the Iran war confronting a crisis that seems to be slipping out of his hands. Global energy prices are surging, the United States stands isolated from allies, and more troops are preparing to deploy despite Trump's promise the war would be only a 'short excursion.' Iran is choking off Gulf oil and gas supplies while launching missile strikes.

Read Full Story →

World News

Three weeks in, Iran war escalates beyond Trump's control

President Donald Trump ends the third week of the Iran war confronting a crisis that seems to be slipping out of his hands. Global energy prices are surging, the United States stands isolated from allies, and more troops are preparing to deploy despite Trump's promise the war would be only a 'short excursion.' Iran is choking off Gulf oil and gas supplies while launching missile strikes.

Reuters · conflict · Read more

Iran fires long-range missiles at Diego Garcia as Trump considers 'winding down' war

Israel confirmed Iranian forces fired long-range ballistic missiles for the first time in the war, expanding the risk of attacks beyond the Middle East. Iran launched two 4,000-kilometer-range missiles at the U.S.-UK military base Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean. Trump stated Washington was considering 'winding down' its military operation against Iran.

Reuters · conflict · Read more

Dozens injured in Israel after Iranian missile strikes target areas near nuclear research center

Iran struck two communities near Israel's main nuclear research center late Saturday, leaving buildings shattered and at least seven people seriously injured. It was the first time Israel's nuclear research center has been targeted in the fighting. Israel's military said it was not able to intercept missiles that hit the southern cities of Dimona and Arad.

Associated Press · conflict · Read more

Iran says US and Israel attacked Natanz nuclear facility

The United States and Israel have struck Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, according to Tehran's atomic energy organisation. The organisation stated that following the 'criminal attacks,' the Natanz enrichment complex was targeted. No leakage of radioactive materials was reported in the area in central Iran.

Al Jazeera · conflict · Read more

Seattle News

49° / 38°
Partly Cloudy
Partly sunny with slight chance of rain

UW Cherry Blossoms Reach Peak Bloom

The University of Washington's iconic cherry blossom trees reached peak bloom on March 20, 2026. The 29 Yoshino cherry trees on the UW Quad are now in full display, marking the arrival of spring in Seattle.

University of Washington / KUOW · Read more

Landslide Blocks Northbound I-5 Near Bellingham

A landslide blocked all northbound lanes of I-5 near Bellingham late Thursday night (March 19), closing the highway just north of North Lake Samish Drive. Approximately 3,000 cubic yards of debris covered the roadway.

FOX 13 Seattle / Bellingham Herald · Read more

Blazing Bagels Closing All Locations Amid Bankruptcy

Beloved Seattle bagel chain Blazing Bagels is closing all locations after filing for Chapter 7 bankruptcy. The company, which operated five locations across the Seattle area, cited rising costs and decreased foot traffic.

Seattle Times / KING 5 · Read more

West Seattle Recycle/Reuse/Shred Event Today

The West Seattle Junction Association is hosting a free recycling and shredding event today from 9 AM to 1 PM at the Junction Plaza Park. Residents can bring documents for shredding and items for recycling.

West Seattle Blog · Read more

Technology

Google expands personalized Gemini AI to all US users

Google is rolling out its Personal Intelligence feature to all US users, allowing Gemini to draw on data from connected apps like Gmail, Photos, and YouTube to deliver more context-aware responses.

MarketingProfs · News · Read more

White House releases AI policy blueprint for Congress

The White House published a long-awaited policy wishlist for federal regulation of artificial intelligence, hoping Congress will codify it into law.

Politico · News · Read more

Anthropic announces 1M token context window for Claude

Anthropic announced general availability of 1 million token context windows for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 at standard pricing, with no beta header and media cap up to 600.

Releasebot · News · Read more

AI startups accounted for 41% of venture capital in 2025

AI startups accounted for 41% of the $128 billion in venture dollars raised by companies on Carta last year — a record-high annual share. VCs have invested over half a trillion dollars into AI over the last five years.

TechCrunch · News · Read more
